text: Persons in dependent positions — migrants, undocumented workers, those with limited literacy, members of minorities — are deceived or coerced into work they would not otherwise accept. Indicators: irregular migration status used as leverage, language isolation, lack of social network, ethnic-minority workers concentrated in lowest-paid roles.

Worker is induced to take up work under false pretences regarding wages, working conditions, employer, location, accommodation, or the legality of the work. Indicators: contract signed in unfamiliar language, job role differs from agreement, wages or hours lower than promised.

Worker is locked into workplace, dormitory, or compound; movement is monitored or controlled; passes required to exit. Indicators: guarded perimeter, dorm curfew, escort to/from shift, no possession of identity documents, exit permission required from manager.

Worker is cut off from family, language community, broader society. Indicators: remote location, no internet/phone access, restricted communication, monolingual workplace among non-native speakers, no contact with home country.

Worker is subjected to violence or sexual abuse, or family members are. Indicators: visible injuries, accounts of beatings, sexual assault by supervisors, threats of violence against children/spouses.

Worker is threatened with denunciation to authorities, dismissal, deportation, loss of housing, withholding of wages, harm to family. Indicators: documented threats, fear-driven testimony, intimidation of workers who complain.

Employer or recruiter holds worker's passport, ID card, work permit, or other essential documents. Indicators: passports stored in HR safe, workers cannot show ID on demand, requests to retrieve documents are denied or delayed.

Wages are not paid in full, not paid at all, paid in kind, paid late, or used to enforce debt repayment. Indicators: pay slips missing, deductions exceed contract, wages paid into accounts worker cannot access, partial payments tied to performance.

Worker is obliged to repay debt through labour, often debt incurred during recruitment (recruitment fees, transport, food, lodging). Indicators: recruitment fees > 1 month wages, ongoing deductions for housing/transport/equipment, debt grows over time, worker cannot leave until debt cleared.

Conditions are degrading and would not be accepted by a free worker. Indicators: unsafe machinery without guards, no PPE, overcrowded dormitories, no toilets or clean water, denial of medical care, dangerous heat/cold exposure.

Worker is required to work beyond legal limits, beyond what is voluntary, beyond what is sustainable. Indicators: > 60 hours/week routine, no weekly rest day, overtime not voluntary, refusal punished by wage deductions or dismissal, peak-season hours exceed 80/week.
